title: Could people see other peoples transition guides
display_title: Could people see other peoples transition guides
wikitext:
[]: Could people see other people's transition guides?             Quote Post by MrMallard » Sat Aug 04, 2012 6:24 am This theory has been bugging me for ages. Let's say you're a denizen in the Evaverse, and Instrumentality has just begun. Now, for some reason, the Rei who breaks through your AT field is late. You're in the same room as several people being assimilated. My question is this: Can you see what their Reis are doing to them, or are you just watching them flip out and explode into orange goo? (Excuse my lack of proper terminology with Rei/Lilith, I really cannot be bothered.)

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Sat Aug 04, 2012 3:09 pm It's possible this happened to Aoba. He's had enough time to retreat under a desk and cower in terror from his (very many) Reis. Either Rei/Lilith took her time with him or he had a chance to run and hide. I'm inclined to think you'd see a Rei, but not someone else's guide (why else does Aoba see so many?). Baseless speculation, though, really.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Aug 04, 2012 3:18 pm It's generally thought Aoba saw all those Reis because he had no one he loved enough, so they had to be a bit more aggressive. Aoba has plenty of reason to cower even without seeing the Reis because all of his coworkers are EXPLODING.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Sat Aug 04, 2012 3:59 pm Like I said, it's base speculation. There's no evidence to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Drabant » Sat Aug 04, 2012 5:17 pm Interesting question. I think it's possible that they do see them, everyone, if only just the Rei/Lilith manifestations and not the person they change into. We certainly don't see any indication that they don't, but then again, we don't see someone getting tanged from someone else's perspective either. What seems likely to me at least, is that these Rei/Lilith manifestations can interact with the physical world, and aren't just visages. Ritsuko types on a keyboard, Misato(Rei/Lilith) pushes back the techie, and so on. You'd have to assume it was all imagination, and then seen from someone elses perspective again if it didn't actually happen. So, if they can move around stuff and so on, I think they might be visible as well, with light reflecting off them and what-not. I doubt Rei/Lilith would have any problems shapeshifting to match expectations either, but then again, it's speculation. The ones that appear before Rei merges with Lilith is another question though, if they can be said to be the same. If they are the same... then I guess both are possible. It does look like only Ritsuko saw Rei for a moment when she died. It's also likely Rei/Lilith can choose whether to be physical or non-physical. If you're speculated to be able to travel time, I think you are capable of a lot else as well. There's no real proof, but I think it's at least tipped in favour that everyone can see them (should Rei/Lilith choose).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 05, 2012 4:15 pm At the very least, the script indicates the Reis turn into whoever the transition guide is (Misato, Yui, Ritsuko): http://evacommentary.org/episode-m26/episode-m26-scene08B.html

[]:             Quote Post by MrMallard » Mon Aug 06, 2012 6:13 am That's the main problem: You never see from the Tanged person's perspective. You see from Hyuga's perspective for a little while, but there's only 1 transition guide to see in the first place. That's what makes this question so hard to answer; there's that lack of information to go by. You can only really go by gut feeling, or by symbolism. There's no middle ground. I'm hoping there's a tiny clue that can answer this theory definitely. EDIT: Star Dust, thank you for that. This is a big help

[]:             Quote Post by deeRez » Mon Aug 06, 2012 9:49 pm The whole point of instrumentality was to chop down walls between people, those pesky self-centered perspectives, and fuse them into one universal perspective. So even if he doesn't see what other people see during tanging, the second he himself becomes tanged, his thoughts are flooded with the thoughts of others, and he comes to know what the other people saw as if he experienced it with his own eyes. How do you think Shinji knew what Misato did with Kaji? Their thoughts got shared with other people.

[]:             Quote Post by MrMallard » Sat Oct 20, 2012 7:25 am EDIT: disregard; I forgot to add why I needed the info. This post is irrelevant.
